summary: iOS | Intermittent | DFU/Language Change fails at >90%
scenario: Device reverts to previous firmware or language after restart despite showing successful DFU or language change completion at >90%
description_clean: Intermittent failure of DFU or Language Change at >90% completion. After DFU, the device shows as up to date but reverts to outdated firmware after restart. After Language Change, the device may briefly show the new language then restart and revert to the previous language. The issue is random and intermittent.
steps: Perform DFU (Device Firmware Update) or Language Change operation on the device.
Observe the operation progress; note that the failure occurs intermittently at >90% completion.
After DFU completes, check the device status — it shows as 'up to date'.
After restart, verify the firmware version — it reverts to the outdated firmware.
Alternatively, after Language Change completes, observe that the device briefly displays the new language.
Wait for the device to restart automatically or manually restart it.
After restart, check the language setting — it reverts to the previous language.
expected: After DFU, the device should remain on the updated firmware after restart. After Language Change, the device should retain the new language after restart.
actual: After DFU, the device shows as up to date but reverts to outdated firmware after restart. After Language Change, the device briefly shows the new language then restarts and reverts to the previous language. The issue is random and intermittent.
